abstract: A pointer to a customized write function that secure transport calls to write data to the connection.
overview:
## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/secure-transport-result-codes].

## Discussion

Before using the secure transport API, you must write the functions [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLReadFunc] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLWriteFunc] and provide them to the library by calling the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetIOFuncs(_:_:_:)] function.

You may configure the underlying connection to operate in a non-blocking manner. In that case, a write operation may well return [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/errSSLWouldBlock], indicating less data than requested was transferred and nothing is wrong except that the requested I/O hasn’t completed. This result is returned to the caller from the functions [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLRead(_:_:_:_:)], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLWrite(_:_:_:_:)], or [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLHandshake(_:)].

# SSLWriteFunc

**Type Alias**

A pointer to a customized write function that secure transport calls to write data to the connection.

## Declaration

```swift
typealias SSLWriteFunc = (SSLConnectionRef, UnsafeRawPointer, UnsafeMutablePointer<Int>) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **connection**: The SSL session connection reference.
- **data**: A pointer to the data to write to the connection.You must allocate this memory before calling this function.
- **dataLength**: Before calling, an integer representing the length of the data in bytes. On return, this is the number of bytes actually transferred.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/secure-transport-result-codes].

## Discussion

Before using the secure transport API, you must write the functions [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLReadFunc] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLWriteFunc] and provide them to the library by calling the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetIOFuncs(_:_:_:)] function.

You may configure the underlying connection to operate in a non-blocking manner. In that case, a write operation may well return [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/errSSLWouldBlock], indicating less data than requested was transferred and nothing is wrong except that the requested I/O hasn’t completed. This result is returned to the caller from the functions [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLRead(_:_:_:_:)], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLWrite(_:_:_:_:)], or [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLHandshake(_:)].
